title: Crisp Fermented German Danmuji Skillet
description: A German-inspired one-pot dish featuring fermented danmuji, crispy textures, and savory flavors, all made in a large tagliere skillet. This nut-free-friendly recipe combines the best of German cuisine with Korean danmuji.
instructions: Heat the vegetable oil in a large tagliere skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the sliced onions and cook until they are translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the sliced carrots and cook for an additional 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the diced potatoes and cook for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until they are tender and lightly browned.
Add the bratwurst sausages and cook until they are browned on all sides and cooked through.
Add the fermented danmuji and cook for 2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Sprinkle the bonito flakes over the top of the skillet and stir to combine.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Cook for an additional 2 minutes, stirring constantly, until the danmuji is crispy and the flavors are combined.
